title: payment_watcher: single-tx multiple outputs to same invoice address cause double-check
description: relay/src/payment_watcher.rs scan_block() iterates over all outputs in all transactions in a block. If a single transaction contains two Sapling outputs to the same invoice address (both decryptable and both meeting the amount threshold), activate_subscription is called once for the first output. The DB atomically deletes the invoice and writes the subscription. For the second output, get_invoice_by_address returns None (invoice was deleted), so the second call is a no-op. This is safe today because activate_subscription_atomic deletes the invoice atomically. However, the behavior is not documented and relies on the atomic delete for correctness. A future change that makes activation non-atomic (e.g., splitting the DB transaction) would silently reintroduce double-activation. Should add an explicit break or dedup after the first successful activation within a tx.
